subject: humanities
name_zh: 美式 MS · 社会研究
description: C3 Framework + State standards. MS typically covers World History, US History, Geography, Civics, and Economics.

domains.name: 世界历史 / World History / World History

nodes.name: 古代文明
curriculum_points: Analyze the influence of geography on the development of political, economic, and social systems in river valley civilizations (e.g., Mesopotamia, Egypt, Indus Valley, China).
Evaluate primary and secondary sources to construct explanations about the cultural achievements and belief systems of ancient civilizations and their enduring legacies.

nodes.name: 古典时代
curriculum_points: Compare and contrast the governmental structures, legal systems, and civic ideals of classical Greece and Rome, and assess their influence on later political thought.

nodes.name: 中世纪世界
curriculum_points: Explain how the interaction of religious institutions, feudal systems, and trade networks (e.g., Silk Roads, Trans-Saharan) shaped societies in Europe, Asia, and Africa during the medieval period.

nodes.name: 近代早期
curriculum_points: Analyze the causes and global consequences of European exploration, including the Columbian Exchange and the development of the Atlantic World.

nodes.name: 现代史
curriculum_points: Evaluate the causes and effects of major political revolutions (e.g., American, French, Haitian) and the rise of nationalism in shaping the modern world.

domains.name: 美国历史 / US History / US History

nodes.name: 殖民地时期
curriculum_points: Construct an argument, using evidence, about the development of self-government and representative institutions in the English colonies, and assess the impact of colonial interactions with Indigenous peoples.

nodes.name: 独立战争与建国
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.8.6-8. Analyze ideas and principles contained in the founding documents of the United States, and explain how they influence the social and political system.

nodes.name: 内战与重建
curriculum_points: Analyze the complex interplay of social, economic, and political factors that led to the Civil War, and evaluate the successes and failures of Reconstruction in achieving its goals.

nodes.name: 工业化时代
curriculum_points: Assess the social, economic, and environmental impacts of industrialization and urbanization, and analyze the resulting reform movements and labor organizations.

nodes.name: 宪法
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.3.6-8. Examine the origins, purposes, and impact of constitutions, laws, treaties, and international agreements.

nodes.name: 三权分立
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.4.6-8. Explain the powers and limits of the three branches of government, public officials, and bureaucracies at different levels in the United States and in other countries.

nodes.name: 权利法案
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.8.6-8. Analyze ideas and principles contained in the founding documents of the United States, and explain how they influence the social and political system.

nodes.name: 公民权利与责任
curriculum_points: D2.Civ.2.6-8. Explain specific roles played by citizens (such as voters, jurors, taxpayers, members of the armed forces, petitioners, protesters, and office-holders).

domains.name: 地理与经济 / Geography & Economics / Geography & Economics

nodes.name: 世界区域
curriculum_points: Apply the Five Themes of Geography (location, place, human-environment interaction, movement, region) to compare the physical and human characteristics of major world regions.

nodes.name: 文化地理
curriculum_points: Investigate how cultural diffusion, migration, and globalization shape and transform cultural landscapes, identities, and patterns of interaction.

nodes.name: 市场经济
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.4.6-8. Describe the role of competition in the determination of prices and wages in a market economy.

nodes.name: 全球经济
curriculum_points: D2.Eco.6.6-8. Explain how changes in supply and demand cause changes in prices and quantities of goods and services, labor, credit, and foreign currencies.
